You guys have worked with 35,000 companies to grow their businesses and you know I would attribute your software to a big chunk of our growth from a quarter million to close to four million now and Continuing to grow and we are just now getting into the segmentation of the the software is really robust So we're just now getting into the nuances of it But even the basic functions of it. Yeah our businesses grow I want to know after dealing with 35,000 companies and helping them grow their businesses. What are best practices? Who's out there winning and how they do it? Yeah, there's a couple of best practices that I would recommend to that just a good starting point for anyone who's trying to get Moving with their email marketing right the first is really want to avoid being a one-trick pony You want to diversify you want to have more than one place for people to sign up more than one type of asset? We get caught up in this idea that we know what our customers want or our prospects want And so if we're not careful We we start pounding on this one note if you've got an e-book if you've got blog posts If you have worksheets or checklists if you have something that appeals to a different again staying with your target customer But something other ways to get them interested avoid the idea of just taking one piece and banging on that one drum You really want to spread yourself out and have different offerings and the great thing is that's also a way to segment your audience Yes, so I know if I'm a financial advisor You know what to do with your first million. Yes. Well, somebody downloads that what do I know about them? They didn't come from a lot of money, but they probably are an entrepreneur They probably made some money and they're ready to do business with me They don't have a financial but if I say how to not ruin your kids by leaving them a bunch of money I know these people are older and I'm gonna on ramp them differently because I said we're in that audience Yeah, absolutely. That's the diversify those leads. Yeah, that's one Another best practice and it sounds very simple is the time of day that you're sending out your content You really want to be careful about the nuances of your particular audience There have been studies out there that show when the best time to send email So for instance if we're sending sending educational content, we might send it Between 8 and midnight 8 p.m. And midnight because people are winding down. They've got the kids down. They're sitting on the couch They're looking through that. They're ready to absorb that now if you're talking about Transactional emails those really it doesn't matter when you're asking them to buy because that's really all about where they are in the buying That one doesn't matter as much But really paying attention to your provider if you're just soft you're able to see when things are opened and wet like what track Their behavior and find out what's actually happening You can run reporting to figure out what that looks like and then send it at appropriate times Also, you can tell what's working. What's not working. What's being downloaded? What's being read who's interacting? You really want to have a system that allows you to do those kinds of things and if you saw does that but Knowing when to send it as a big big part of it Okay, diversify the lead generators and know when to send the email pay attention to the times that go out What else the third one that I wanted to share with you has to do with is write up your alley is keep it simple Yeah, you know We're we sometimes we get into this mindset that we want to share everything and I know with this story brand you talk about that You're getting down and being clear, but people are we're competing for all kinds of things And so their time their energy and so keeping it very simple very brief is gonna help you get opens and get responses in your email You want to say everything you want to say you don't want to be like that Facebook friend that says I'm hurting today pray for me, you know, you really want to give them enough information. They know what they're doing But you don't want to overwhelm them because they won't read it I I've struggled with that and I had to learn the hard way. I had a manager said to me. Listen, I'm sorry. I didn't read your email It's too long Alright, so I wanted to say that to many people Yeah, so those are the three big takeaways that I would keep it brief get to the point make the email enjoyable to read Use the pastor framework that you learn in this course to write some of those emails and people will actually read them Use a narrative idea start with a problem if that opening sentence or paragraph is about somebody's problem Or a lot of people struggle with this we all know the pain of this They're hooked why because that's how a story hooks you it hooks you with a person with a problem If you have a story go to a movie the hero shows up and they don't have any sort of problem We're all checking out of that movie. We got to get to that problem I get emails from folks from businesses and I've actually called some of them because you know They're not even clients, but I just called the hey I kind of just really if you just to put a call to action at the bottom of that email I'd have bought it but you just you talked about this problem We're all having this problem and you know here's something you can do to fix it and then I'm gonna go buy that at Walgreens I'm not gonna buy from you because you didn't actually put You know little things like that We've got to think through those emails and make them interesting and call people to action Start with a problem offer a resolution keep it really brief and simple You know it's basic stuff, but the bottom line Francis, and I think you'd agree with me is get started Yeah, get moving on this start sending email That is the biggest thing is I can't tell you a number of customers that have come to our infuse off University or come to an event we've held and they said they've owned it for a couple years and they're Almost ready to launch something because they're version one is better than version none and you've got to get started Even if it's just a single standalone email You'll be shocked at the results you get off just engaging with them a little bit and that feeds the Desire to do more and that feeds the desire to do more and you'll be rolling in no time Let's give people before we sign off on this teaching module. Let's you and I give people permission to Screw up because they're probably going to make some mistakes Yeah, and I'm telling you I've got a great company. It's really successful. I have sent out typos Yeah, I have said out you're going to make mistakes a lot of you're going I can't do it's gonna be perfect Well, you know people are very more forgiving. Now this is not excusing Any lack of excellence we've got to be excellent what we do. I remember one time we had this meeting where you know We're going to get 10,000 new leads this month That's our goal and I think two days later We actually sent out a blank email to our entire list but at the bottom it said to unsubscribe from this list because legally You know it's automatic automatically put in there And so basically said let's get 10,000 new emails then we emailed our entire list and asked them to unsubscribe But we're still alive We're still here. We're still moving you're gonna make some mistakes But it's a fun process but get started right away get started right actually It's a pretty well-known marketing technique that when you make a mistake sending a follow-up email. It says oops You made a mistake. Yeah, it's open rate on those is enormous is people want to find out what the mistake was so well There you go make a mistake admit it tell everybody what happened. There you go Listen get started right away Francis was a long flight for you to come out here We're very very grateful so excited input and a lot of companies are gonna grow because of what you shared with us Awesome.
